input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10.

This is a known change in .NET MAUI. In .NET 10 (which targets Android 15+ / API 35), edge-to-edge display became the default, and `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated in Android API 30 and fully removed in API 35.

The solution in .NET 10 is to use the new keyboard insets handling. Let me provide the correct guidance.


In .NET 10 with Android 15, `adjustResize` is no longer functional when targeting API 35+, so the modern approach is to handle keyboard visibility through `WindowInsets` and the IME insets system rather than relying on the deprecated resize mode.

For a practical solution in MAUI 10, I can either use the built-in `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` that's been available since .NET 8, or manually listen to IME insets and adjust padding accordingly—the latter being more suitable for chat screens where the entire layout needs to shift rather than just scrolling to the focused element.
